shared_properties: Project core palette keeps primary #D6D0C4, background #0D0D0F, and accent #E07B39.
Canvas target stays 320x180px with pixel-perfect presentation at 60 FPS.
Every painted edge keeps flat pixel clusters that stay readable under a 4x upscale.
differences: Backdrop spans 320x540px so one authored sheet covers three camera heights before repeating.
Depth layers separate abyss black, deep slate, and dungeon stone bands to support 0.4x parallax motion.
Composition leaves the center lane visually open so the falling route stays readable without any progress meter.

context.usage: Parallax backdrop for the vertical prison shaft during continuous descent.

shape.silhouette: Layered stone void with repeating alcove rhythm.
shape.collision_read: Background only with no collision pixels.

acceptance_criteria: The top and bottom 180px bands loop without a visible seam when the camera scrolls downward.
No numerals, arrows, or progress markers appear anywhere in the backdrop art.

reference_images.label: slip_down_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds stone texture breakup, low-key lighting, and vertical shaft composition for the active environment asset.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteBundle on a dedicated parallax layer with a repeating material region.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load once during the Loading state and keep the handle in a background resource used by the shaft camera system.
runtime_notes: Use Bevy 0.13 with ImagePlugin::default_nearest() so every asset stays pixel-perfect at 4x upscale.
Scroll this backdrop at 0.4x camera speed so foreground collision art stays readable.
Anchor the image to whole pixels before parallax offset is applied.
